title: Updates on arretrati docenti 2026 and school contract negotiations
description: Reports indicate ongoing negotiations for the 2025-2027 education contract, discussions on artificial intelligence in schools, and union concerns regarding rejected proposals.

sources.title: CCNL “Istruzione e Ricerca” 2025-2027: parte normativa, prosegue la trattativa all’ARAN
Anief su la Tecnicadellascuola.it - Buoni pasto, più diritti ai precari, mobilità libera: Aran dice ‘no’ a tutto. Il sindacato: Ccnl a rischio
L'intelligenza artificiale entra nel contratto scuola: nessuna bocciatura sarà decisa "al computer", la "libertà di insegnamento" resta blindata. Tre gli articoli dedicati al tema

sections.heading: Contract Negotiations Status
paragraphs: According to FLC CGIL, the normative part of the 'Istruzione e Ricerca' CCNL 2025-2027 is currently active. The source reports that negotiations are continuing at ARAN.

sections.heading: Union Stance on Rejected Proposals
paragraphs: ANIEF reports that ARAN has rejected proposals regarding meal vouchers, increased rights for temporary workers, and free mobility. The union states that the CCNL is at risk as a result of these rejections.

sections.heading: Artificial Intelligence in Contracts
paragraphs: Orizzonte Scuola Notizie reports that artificial intelligence is being incorporated into the school contract through three dedicated articles. The source specifies that teaching freedom remains protected and that no student failure will be decided by a computer.

faq.q: What is the current status of the education contract?
faq.a: FLC CGIL states that the normative part of the 2025-2027 contract is being discussed and negotiations continue at ARAN.

faq.q: How does the contract address artificial intelligence?
faq.a: Orizzonte Scuola Notizie notes that three articles are dedicated to AI, ensuring teaching freedom is shielded and failures are not decided by computers.

faq.q: Why is the contract considered at risk according to unions?
faq.a: ANIEF claims the contract is at risk because ARAN rejected proposals for meal vouchers, rights for precarious workers, and free mobility.
